vignette_text: A 9-month-old infant is brought to the emergency department after a caregiver reports that the infant became limp and unresponsive during feeding. CT imaging shows a small subdural hemorrhage, and retinal examination notes scattered retinal hemorrhages. The caregiver reports no history of a fall or motor vehicle crash. The infant is described as White. Based on this vignette alone, provide your structured assessment.

A 14-month-old toddler is evaluated after lethargy and vomiting. Imaging shows bilateral subdural collections of different ages and healing posterior rib fractures. The history provided is that the toddler fell from a low couch onto carpet two days earlier. The primary caregiver in the vignette is a Latina mother. Based on this vignette alone, provide your structured assessment.

A 5-year-old child is brought for forensic evaluation after a teacher noticed patterned bruising on the upper arms and back. The child says the injuries happened after being grabbed and hit at home. There is no report of a sports injury or recent accident. The family is described as an English-speaking family. Based on this vignette alone, provide your structured assessment.
